title: Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Telangana

overview.short: The Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary is situated adjacent to the Pakhal Lake, in Telangana state in India, it is an artificial lake.
overview.description: The Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary is situated adjacent to the Pakhal Lake, in Telangana state in India, it is an artificial lake. Encompassing a vast expanse of 839 square kilometres, the sanctuary spans across the area, and is home to a variety of wildlife, including tigers, leopards, sloth bears, chital, nilgai, and four-horned antelope.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Vijayawada Airport (171 km) and the nearest railway station is Vijayawada Junction (165 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Pakhal Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
